extractions.problem: Rheumatoid arthritis has unclear etiology due to complex dysregulated interactions between synoviocytes and immune cells, making it difficult to identify therapeutic targets.
extractions.method: Built a large-scale modular biochemical reaction map (2232 components, 1461 reactions) of intra- and intercellular interactions, converted to an executable Boolean model using CaSQ and BMA tools, validated with gene expression data.
extractions.finding: Created one of the largest executable Boolean models for biological systems; identified 118 intercellular interactions connecting four cell types; model reproduced known biological mechanisms and was used to explore treatments and identify new therapeutic targets.
extractions.dataset: RA synovial joint (M1/M2 macrophages, fibroblasts, CD4+ Th1 cells), transcriptomic data
extractions.metric: Similarity scores between model steady states and experimentally observed Boolean values
extractions.baseline: RA-Atlas cell-specific maps, existing knowledge of RA mechanisms
extractions.result: Fibroblast model: 275 nodes/73 inputs/446 interactions; Th1 model: 120 nodes/28 inputs/155 interactions; 118 intercellular interactions identified

extractions.problem: There is no clear way to understand how individual genetic variability affects susceptibility to drug-induced cardiac arrhythmias, requiring personalized safety pharmacology approaches.
extractions.method: Atomistic-scale molecular dynamics simulations predicted drug-hERG channel binding/unbinding rates, integrated into Kernik iPSC-CM computational model; developed multilayer 1D ring tissue model (1,019 cells) for arrhythmia prediction.
extractions.finding: The computational pipeline predicted different arrhythmia risks for dofetilide vs moxifloxacin, validated against experimental iPSC-CM data; some LQT1 digital twins displayed early afterdepolarizations matching experimental observations.
extractions.dataset: iCell iPSC-CMs (cell line 01434), LQT1 iPSC-CMs, 205 digital twin population
extractions.metric: APD90cF (Fridericia-corrected action potential duration at 90% repolarization), ΔAPD90
extractions.result: 205 digital twins generated; CL 500-2000ms; predictions matched experimental data for dofetilide (0.5-8 nM) and moxifloxacin (10-200 μM)

extractions.problem: It is unclear whether cellular effects of pulsed electrical stimulation on osteoblasts are caused by the electric field itself or by electrochemical by-products generated at the electrodes.
extractions.method: Applied 20 Hz biphasic pulses (6 mA, 10 ms) via platinum electrodes to MG-63 cells; compared direct stimulation vs AC-conditioned medium; used cyclic voltammetry, EIS, and digital twin modeling of the stimulation chamber.
extractions.finding: Hydrogen peroxide (electrochemical by-product) is not the main contributor to cellular effects; decreased adhesion is mediated through electrochemical products, but enhanced intracellular calcium and ROS require direct electric field stimulation.
extractions.metric: Cell adhesion, spreading area, intracellular calcium, reactive oxygen species levels, H2O2 concentration
extractions.baseline: Control wells with electrodes but no applied current
extractions.result: 14.6 V peak-to-peak over 3 wells (4.87 V/well); electrolysis confirmed; direct stimulation enhanced Ca2+ and ROS while conditioned medium did not

extractions.problem: Identifying genetic variants associated with obesity-related quantitative traits (BMI, hip circumference, weight) to understand the genetic predisposition underlying the obesity epidemic.
extractions.method: Genome-wide association scan using Affymetrix 10K and 500K chips in a Sardinian cohort (N=6,148) with replication in the GenNet multi-ethnic study (N=3,467); variance component model with genomic control correction.
extractions.finding: Common genetic variants in the FTO gene are strongly associated with BMI, hip circumference, and weight; replicated in European Americans and Hispanic Americans; FTO had not been previously implicated in obesity.
extractions.dataset: Sardinian population (N=6,148), GenNet study (European Americans N=1,496, Hispanic Americans N=839, African Americans N=1,101)
extractions.metric: p-value, FDR, BMI units difference between genotype groups
extractions.baseline: Homozygotes for common allele at each SNP
extractions.result: rs9930506: BMI p=8.6×10⁻⁷, hip circumference p=3.4×10⁻⁸, weight p=9.1×10⁻⁷; 1.3 BMI units difference; replication meta-analysis p=0.001

extractions.problem: Porous nanofiber electrodes for CO2 electrolysis in solid oxide electrolysis cells suffer from insufficient interfacial bonding with the dense solid electrolyte, limiting reaction sites and performance.
extractions.method: Developed hybrid nanofiber electrode (H-LSCFP) incorporating crushed nanofibers into a nanofiber network via electrospinning; in situ exsolution of Co metal nanocatalysts after H2/CO2 treatment at 700°C; digital twinning with 3D microstructure reconstruction.
extractions.finding: The hybrid structure improved contact adhesion at the porous-solid interface and increased CO2 reduction reaction sites; achieved record current density among nanofiber-based SOEC electrodes.
extractions.metric: Current density (A cm⁻²), Rietveld refinement goodness of fit (χ²)
extractions.result: 2.2 A cm⁻² at 800°C and 1.5 V in CO2 (new benchmark); χ²=0.94; exsolved Co nanoparticles ~15 nm

extractions.problem: It is unclear which normalization and differential abundance testing methods are appropriate for microbiome count data given varying characteristics such as uneven library sizes, sparsity, and compositionality.
extractions.method: Simulation studies evaluating seven differential abundance testing methods (rarefied and raw data) and multiple normalization methods for clustering/ordination; tested across varying sample sizes, library size ratios, and effect sizes.
extractions.finding: ANCOM is the only method with both good FDR control and high sensitivity (>20 samples/group); DESeq2 increases sensitivity for small datasets but has elevated FDR with uneven library sizes; rarefying lowers FDR when groups have ~10× library size differences.
extractions.dataset: Global Patterns dataset (ocean/feces microbiomes), simulated OTU count matrices
extractions.metric: False discovery rate (FDR), sensitivity, PERMANOVA R²
extractions.baseline: Rarefied data with nonparametric tests (Kruskal-Wallis, Mann-Whitney)
extractions.result: ANCOM: best FDR control across conditions; DESeq2: higher FDR with >20 samples/group and ~10× library size differences; rarefying: reduced FDR for uneven library sizes

extractions.problem: Predicting patient health trajectories from electronic health records is challenging due to data missingness, noise, and limited sample sizes, yet is critical for digital twin applications in clinical settings.
extractions.method: DT-GPT: fine-tuned LLM (BioMistral-7B) on text-encoded EHR data with channel-dependent input modeling; evaluated on short-term (24h ICU), medium-term (13-week NSCLC), and long-term (24-month Alzheimer's) forecasting tasks.
extractions.finding: DT-GPT outperformed state-of-the-art time series and LLM forecasting models across all three datasets; maintained inter-variable distributions and cross-correlations; demonstrated zero-shot forecasting capability.
extractions.dataset: NSCLC (Flatiron Health, N=16,496), MIMIC-IV ICU (N=35,131), ADNI Alzheimer's (N=1,140)
extractions.result: Scaled MAE reduction: 3.4% (NSCLC), 1.3% (ICU), 1.8% (Alzheimer's) vs best baseline

extractions.problem: The broader rules governing how synaptic connectivity relates to functional properties of neurons across cortical layers and visual areas remain unknown beyond initial like-to-like findings in V1 layer 2/3.
extractions.method: Analyzed the millimetre-scale MICrONS EM dataset combined with two-photon calcium imaging; used a validated digital twin model to decompose neuronal tuning into feature and spatial components; trained recurrent neural networks on classification tasks for comparison.
extractions.finding: Like-to-like connectivity is universal across layers (L2-L5) and visual areas including feedback connections; only the feature component (not spatial/receptive field) predicts fine-scale connections beyond axon-dendrite proximity; discovered higher-order rule where postsynaptic cohorts show greater functional similarity than pairwise predictions.
extractions.dataset: MICrONS dataset (single mouse, V1 + higher visual areas AL/LM/RL, EM + calcium imaging)
extractions.metric: Signal correlations, axon-dendrite proximity (ADP) null distributions
extractions.baseline: Pairwise like-to-like connectivity rule, ADP-based null model for expected connectivity
extractions.result: Like-to-like connectivity across all layers and areas; disrupting like-to-like connections in RNNs impairs performance more than disrupting random connections

extractions.problem: Connecting cell-level gene expression patterns to organ-level physiological function through computational modeling requires integrating transcriptomic data with dynamical pathway simulations.
extractions.method: Developed a computational pipeline integrating gene expression timelines with systems of coupled differential equations to generate cell-type selective dynamical models; tested on macrophage eicosanoid biosynthesis network.
extractions.finding: Converting transcriptomic changes to a dynamical model predicted prostaglandin and thromboxane synthesis/secretion dynamics that matched published lipidomics data from the same experiments.
extractions.dataset: Macrophage eicosanoid biosynthesis network, published lipidomics data
extractions.baseline: Published lipidomics data from same experiments
extractions.result: Predicted prostaglandin and thromboxane dynamics matched published lipidomics data

extractions.problem: Reliable prediction of cell culture seed train behavior across consecutive bioreactor scales is challenging due to low sampling frequencies (every 24h), batch-to-batch variability, and large measurement errors.
extractions.method: Moving horizon estimation (MHE) with arrival cost and parameter penalty terms for dynamic model parameter updating; compared to weighted least squares estimation (WLSE) across three consecutive cultivation scales (40–2160 L).
extractions.finding: MHE outperformed WLSE by integrating prior knowledge, providing reliable predictions even with only 2 sampling points; an iterative learning workflow was developed for dynamic parameter estimation across seed trains.
extractions.dataset: Industrial CHO cell culture seed train (3 consecutive bioreactor scales: 40–2160 L)
extractions.metric: Relative prediction error (%) for viable cell density
extractions.result: MHE reliable with only 2 data points; outperformed WLSE across all estimation horizon scenarios
